faqs.name: FAQs — Aetas in the Workplace for Charities

mainEntity.name: What is an Investment Policy Statement and do we legally need one?

acceptedAnswer.text: An Investment Policy Statement (IPS) is a formal document setting out your charity's investment objectives, risk appetite, ethical restrictions, and governance framework. If your charity has delegated discretionary investment management to an external manager, an IPS is a legal requirement under Charity Commission guidance.

mainEntity.name: What is an OCF and why does it matter?

acceptedAnswer.text: An Ongoing Charges Figure (OCF) is a fee embedded within investment funds, deducted directly from fund assets. It does not appear as a visible line on a statement and is frequently not raised in trustee meetings. Over time, OCF charges can represent a material drag on net returns.

mainEntity.name: Is this affordable for a not-for-profit?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial discovery conversation is provided at no cost. If further work is recommended, fees are transparent and agreed in advance. In many cases, improvements identified through a benefits and investment review generate savings that offset the cost of the engagement.

mainEntity.name: We already have an Employee Assistance Programme. Does that cover financial wellbeing?

acceptedAnswer.text: An EAP typically provides reactive, crisis-focused support. It does not review your benefits structure, run financial resilience workshops, or provide one-to-one guidance from a regulated financial planner. Aetas ITW sits alongside an EAP, addressing proactive financial capability building.
